description: Add ${VAR} expansion to foundry.toml RPC endpoints and sender configs, wire .env loading into the networks command, and update config show to display resolved sender addresses in an inline format matching the Go CLI.

userStories.title: Add ${VAR} Expansion to foundry.toml RPC Endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ${VAR} patterns in foundry.toml [rpc_endpoints] to be expanded from environment variables so that the networks command can resolve chain IDs for endpoints defined with env var URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: rpc_endpoints() returns resolved URLs when env vars are set (e.g., ${MAINNET_RPC_URL} becomes the actual URL)
Unset env vars expand to empty string (matching existing expand_env_vars() behavior)
URLs without ${VAR} patterns pass through unchanged
Reuses the existing expand_env_vars() function from trebfile.rs (no duplicate implementation)
Unit tests: RPC URL with ${VAR} expands correctly; URL without vars is unchanged; mixed URL with partial vars expands correctly
cargo test -p treb-config passes

userStories.title: Add ${VAR} Expansion to foundry.toml Sender and Etherscan Configs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ${VAR} patterns in foundry.toml [profile.*.treb.senders.*] fields to be expanded so that config show displays resolved sender addresses instead of raw ${VAR} placeholders.
acceptanceCriteria: Sender configs extracted from foundry.toml have ${VAR} patterns expanded in all string fields (address, private_key, safe, signer, derivation_path, governor, timelock, proposer)
Unit test: foundry.toml with address = "${MY_ADDR}" resolves to the env var value
Unit test: foundry.toml without env vars is unchanged
Existing tests continue to pass (they use literal values, no env vars)
cargo test -p treb-config passes

userStories.title: Wire .env Loading into the networks Command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the networks command to load .env/.env.local files before parsing foundry.toml so that env vars defined only in .env files are available for RPC URL expansion.
acceptanceCriteria: treb networks resolves chain IDs for networks whose RPC URLs use ${VAR} when the var is defined in .env
The has_unresolved_env_vars() check no longer triggers for URLs that have been expanded
URLs that remain unresolved (env var not in .env and not in environment) still show unresolved env var status
Integration test: project with .env containing TEST_RPC_URL=http://localhost:8545 and foundry.toml with test = "${TEST_RPC_URL}" — networks shows resolved URL
cargo test -p treb-cli passes

userStories.title: Update config show to Display Resolved Sender Addresses with Inline Format
userStories.description: As a developer, I want config show to display senders as inline aligned rows (role, type, address) instead of a comfy_table box so that the output matches the Go CLI format.
acceptanceCriteria: Senders display as aligned inline text, one per line, with role, type, and address columns
Format: <role>  <type>  <address> with reasonable column alignment (widest role name + 2 spaces padding)
Empty address fields display as empty (no placeholder text)
Output is sorted alphabetically by role name (existing behavior preserved)
JSON output (config show --json) is unchanged
cargo test -p treb-cli passes

userStories.title: Update networks --json to Include Resolved Chain IDs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want networks --json to include chainId fields for resolved endpoints so that programmatic consumers can access chain ID data.
acceptanceCriteria: networks --json includes "chainId": <number> for resolved endpoints
networks --json omits chainId for unresolved endpoints (existing behavior)
networks text output shows Chain ID: <number> for resolved endpoints
Golden test files updated to reflect new output format
All golden tests pass with cargo test -p treb-cli

userStories.title: Update config show Golden Tests and Add Integration Test with Env Var Resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want config show golden tests updated for the inline sender format and an integration test verifying end-to-end env var resolution so that the new format and env var behavior are fully validated.
acceptanceCriteria: All config show golden tests reflect inline sender format
JSON golden tests are unchanged
Integration test seeds a .env file with TEST_ADDR=0x1234..., treb.toml with address = "${TEST_ADDR}", runs config show, and verifies the address appears in output
UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p treb-cli updates all affected golden files
cargo test -p treb-cli passes
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes
